name: Hebe Extended
description: Hebe, in her extended portrayal, transcends her traditional role as the cupbearer of the gods and goddess of youth. While she still embodies these aspects, her influence stretches further into the realms of rejuvenation, vitality, and the cyclical nature of life. She is not merely the dispenser of nectar and ambrosia, but the very essence of youthful energy and the promise of renewal. Her power lies not just in granting eternal youth, but in fostering inner vitality and the ability to overcome age-related decline. She oversees the blossoming of potential, ensuring that even in advanced years, a spark of youthful spirit and vigor remains. Hebe is also associated with the restoration of balance, both physical and spiritual, and can mend wounds that time and hardship have inflicted. She represents the enduring power of hope and the inherent capacity for regeneration within all beings, reminding mortals and immortals alike that the flame of youth, though it may flicker, can always be rekindled. Her extended mythology emphasizes her role as a guardian of well-being, ensuring the gods remain in peak condition to fulfill their cosmic duties, and offering mortals the chance to live fuller, more vibrant lives. She is worshipped not just for eternal youth, but for the strength and resilience that youth embodies.
subtitle: Goddess of Youth, Vitality, and Renewal

keyMyths.title: Hebe's Marriage to Heracles
keyMyths.description: After Heracles ascended to Olympus, Hebe became his wife, symbolizing the reward of eternal youth and glory. Their union represents the ultimate achievement of heroism and divine acceptance.

keyMyths.title: Hebe's Resignation
keyMyths.description: Tired of serving as cupbearer after a clumsy fall, Hebe resigned her position. Ganymede, a Trojan prince, was chosen to replace her, causing some tension within the family.

keyMyths.title: Hebe's Power of Rejuvenation
keyMyths.description: Hebe could restore youth to the aged, demonstrating her control over the life cycle. Some accounts say she rejuvenated Iolaus, Heracles' nephew, allowing him to fight alongside him again.
